articles.headline: 2026 World Cup: Which 48 teams qualified for the tournament?
articles.description: The race to qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is complete. Here's how it all went down across the globe.

images.name: Marcotti: Italy have 'no excuses' for another World Cup failure

article.headline: Brazil thrash Bolivia 5-1 in Neymar's historic appearance
article.description: Brazil lived up to expectations by smashing Bolivia 5-1 at home in their 2026 World Cup qualifiers opener on Friday, with Neymar surpassing the late great Pele as the country's top scorer.
article.linkText: Brazil thrash Bolivia 5-1 in Neymar's historic appearance

images.name: Neymar 'happy' on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after overtaking Pele
images.caption: Neymar shares his emotions on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after netting twice in the 5-1 over Bolivia.

video.headline: Neymar 'happy' on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after overtaking Pele
video.caption: Neymar shares his emotions on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after netting twice in the 5-1 over Bolivia.
video.title: Neymar 'happy' on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after overtaking Pele
video.description: Neymar shares his emotions on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after netting twice in the 5-1 over Bolivia.

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC_CONMEBOL News (Neymar happy on becoming Brazils all time mens top scorer after overtaking Pele) 20230909 GLOBAL

article.story:
Brazil lived up to expectations by smashing Bolivia 5-1 at home in their 2026 World Cup qualifiers opener on Friday, with Neymar surpassing the late great Pele as the country's top scorer.

The 31-year-old Neymar, who recently joined Saudi side Al-Hilal, bounced back after missing a penalty in the first half, scoring two goals in the 61st and 93rd minutes to become the country's all-time top scorer with 79 goals.

Real Madrid's Rodrygo also shone with a double, opening the scoring in the 24th minute and bagging his second in the 52nd off a great through ball from Newcastle United's Bruno Guimaraes for 3-0.

Raphinha, who replaced Antony in the Brazilian squad, scored to make it 2-0 in the opening seconds of the second half with a shot at the far post after picking up Neymar's pass in the edge of the box.

Bolivia got their consolation goal through Victor Abrego's powerful shot in the 78th minute.

Brazil will look to continue their good form when they visit Peru on Tuesday in the second round of CONMEBOL qualifiers.

Elsewhere, Uruguay started their qualifying campaign on the right foot by beating Chile 3-1 at home to hand former Leeds United coach Marcelo Bielsa his first official win at the helm.

videos.headline: Neymar 'happy' on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after overtaking Pele
videos.description: Neymar shares his emotions on becoming Brazil's all-time men's top scorer after netting twice in the 5-1 over Bolivia.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Penalty saved! Neymar (Brazil) fails to capitalise on this great opportunity,  right footed shot saved  in the bottom left corner.
Marcelo Moreno (Bolivia) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Neymar (Brazil) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Brazil 1, Bolivia 0. Rodrygo (Brazil) left footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner.
First Half ends, Brazil 1, Bolivia 0.
Goal!  Brazil 2, Bolivia 0. Raphinha (Brazil) left footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Neymar.

Substitution, Bolivia. Carlos Roca replaces Roberto Fernández.

Substitution, Brazil. Ibañez replaces Gabriel Magalhães because of an injury.
Substitution, Brazil. Gabriel Jesus replaces Rodrygo.
Goal!  Brazil 5, Bolivia 1. Neymar (Brazil) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Raphinha.
Second Half ends, Brazil 5, Bolivia 1.
